back: A device that converts digital data from a computer into a form that can be seen, heard or acted upon.
Speaker, printer, monitor, projector, actuator.
It converts digital sound data into analogue sound.
Digital-to-Analogue Converter.
To convert digital sound data into analogue signals that can be heard.
A device that converts an electrical signal into physical movement.
Motors, valves, robotic arms, central locking systems.

front: How does an inkjet printer work?
back: It sprays tiny droplets of ink onto paper.

front: When is an inkjet printer typically used?
back: For high-quality colour printing such as photos.

front: How does a laser printer work?
back: It uses a laser and toner powder fused onto paper using heat.

front: When is a laser printer typically used?
back: For fast, high-volume printing in offices.
A device that creates physical objects layer by layer from a digital design.
Building objects by adding material layer by layer.
Creating objects by removing material from a solid block.
A display that uses liquid crystals and requires a backlight.
A display that uses LEDs to produce light directly.

front: Give one advantage of LED over LCD.
back: Brighter images and lower power usage.
A projector that uses LCD panels and coloured light.
A projector that uses microscopic mirrors to reflect light.

front: Give one difference between DLP and LCD projectors.
back: DLP projectors are smaller but noisier, LCD projectors have better image quality.
